skill.name: twitter-automation
skill.description: Automate Twitter/X with posting, engagement, and user management via inference.sh CLI. Post tweets, schedule content, like posts, retweet, send DMs, follow users, and get profiles.
skill.summary: Automate Twitter/X posting, engagement, and user management via inference.sh CLI
skill.author: tul-sh

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The skill is a legitimate Twitter/X automation tool that uses the inference.sh CLI to make API calls. The detected 'shell commands' are markdown code blocks in documentation, not executable code. URLs are documentation links, not exfiltration endpoints. No actual security risks identified.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Shell Command Detection in Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner detected backtick syntax at multiple lines. These are markdown code fences showing bash command examples, not Ruby/shell execution. Commands use hardcoded app IDs with JSON input parameters - no user input concatenation or dynamic command building.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Backticks in markdown documentation are code fence delimiters, not executable code. No actual shell execution vectors present.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Network URL Detection
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner detected hardcoded URLs. These are legitimate documentation and reference URLs (inference.sh, cloud storage, docs), not credential exfiltration or malicious network calls.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: URLs are documentation links to inference.sh service - standard reference URLs, not suspicious network activity.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Cryptographic Algorithm Detection
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner incorrectly flagged YAML frontmatter and markdown table content as 'weak cryptographic algorithm'. No cryptographic code exists in this documentation file.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is YAML frontmatter name field, line 27 is markdown table. No cryptographic algorithms present.

content.value_statement: Automate Twitter/X posting, likes, retweets, DMs, and follows using the inference.sh CLI. Perfect for content creators and social media managers who need to schedule and manage their social presence.
actual_capabilities: Get user profiles and tweet details
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI and account setup
Rate limits depend on Twitter/X API and inference.sh service
Cannot manage lists or communities directly
Media must be hosted externally with accessible URLs

use_cases.title: Content Creator Posting
use_cases.description: Automate posting AI-generated images and videos to Twitter. Generate content with AI tools then immediately share to your audience.
use_cases.target_user: Content creators, AI artists, digital marketers

use_cases.title: Social Media Management
use_cases.description: Schedule and automate engagement activities like liking relevant posts, following target accounts, and sending welcome DMs to new followers.

use_cases.title: Automated Customer Support
use_cases.description: Monitor mentions and send automated responses or DMs to customers engaging with your brand account.

prompt_templates.title: Post a Simple Tweet
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the twitter-automation skill to post a tweet that says: [YOUR TWEET TEXT HERE]

prompt_templates.title: Post with AI-Generated Image
prompt_templates.prompt: First generate an image using the AI image tool, then use twitter-automation to post it with the caption: [YOUR CAPTION]

prompt_templates.title: Engage with Target Account
prompt_templates.prompt: Use twitter-automation to: 1) Get the profile of [USERNAME], 2) Like their latest 3 tweets, 3) Follow them

prompt_templates.title: Automated Content Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up an automated workflow that: 1) Generates content using [CONTENT_TOOL], 2) Posts to Twitter using twitter-automation with relevant hashtags

output_examples.input: Post a tweet saying 'Just launched our new product! Check it out at example.com'
output_examples.output: Successfully posted tweet. Tweet ID: 1234567890, Posted at: 2024-01-15T10:30:00Z
Successfully liked tweet 9876543210. Like count updated.

best_practices: Always review automated messages before sending to avoid reputation damage
Space out engagement actions to stay within Twitter rate limits
Use media hosted on reliable CDNs to ensure images/videos load correctly
anti_patterns: Do not spam follow/unfollow in quick succession - this triggers Twitter penalties
Avoid posting identical content multiple times - use unique variations
Do not send unsolicited DMs - this violates Twitter policies and annoys users

faq.question: Do I need a Twitter Developer account?
faq.answer: No. This skill uses inference.sh which handles the Twitter API integration. You just need an inference.sh account.

faq.question: Can I schedule tweets for later?
faq.answer: This skill posts immediately. For scheduling, you would need to implement your own delay logic or use an external scheduler.

faq.question: Is this against Twitter terms of service?
faq.answer: Using automation tools can violate Twitter policies if used for spam or abuse. Use responsibly and within Twitter guidelines.

faq.question: What rate limits should I expect?
faq.answer: Rate limits depend on both Twitter API limits and inference.sh service limits. Start with slow actions and monitor.

faq.question: Can I delete tweets with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, use the x/post-delete app with a tweet_id to delete specific tweets.

faq.question: How do I get media URLs to attach to tweets?
faq.answer: Host images on services like Cloudinary, S3, or GitHub. Provide the direct publicly-accessible URL in the media_url field.
